curator_log.drop_reason: Three independent corruptions in the canonical patch make this task unscoreable: (1) Prefix mismatch — instruction.md and tests/test.sh require underscore-style filenames (01_microtokenizer.py, 03_microgpt.py, ...) while canonical_patches/809e358e... uses dash-style (03-microgpt.py, 04-micrornn.py, 01_01-microtokenizer.py). The verifier's regex (01_microtokenizer|02_microembedding|...) and file existence checks would zero out a session-faithful agent. (2) 11 REDACTED ops — _reconstruction_warnings lists ops 23, 35–41, 60, 123, 128 all targeting REDACTED.md or non-existent renamed files; the canonical reconstruction is missing roughly a quarter of the agent's edits. (3) Double-prefix corruption — the patch literally writes `01_01-microtokenizer.py` into README.md (line ~80) and docs/implementation.md tree, indicating broken find-and-replace state. Additionally, files_changed shows only 3 doc files modified with zero `git mv` operations — none of the 7 .py renames the plan calls for actually appear in the patch. No coherent benchmark unit; matches prior batch DROP.
curator_log.notes: Verified prior batch DROP. Canonical patch artifact: data-pipeline/artifacts_unknown/canonical_patches/809e358e-f9d8-4d9d-a17a-6cfc524cc4b6.json. Patch summary: 3 files (README.md, docs/autograd-interface.md, docs/implementation.md), +27/-27, no .py renames. Instruction/test require underscore filenames; patch uses dashes; both schemes co-exist in the patch with one file double-prefixed (01_01-microtokenizer.py). 43 mutating ops attempted, 11 hit missing/REDACTED targets.
